close_reason: Completed: persist settings (runtime, max_concurrent, auto_spawn, poll_interval_secs, model per runtime) to obelisk.toml on exit; load on startup
description:
All obelisk settings reset to hardcoded defaults on every restart. The user must re-select their preferred runtime, model, max_concurrent, auto_spawn, and poll_interval every time they launch the application.

**Current behavior (app.rs:241-277):**
- selected_runtime defaults to ClaudeCode
- max_concurrent defaults to 10
- auto_spawn defaults to false
- poll_interval_secs defaults to 30
- model_indices defaults to index 0 for all runtimes
- No config file is read or written

**Expected behavior:**
- On startup, read an obelisk config file (e.g., .beads/obelisk.toml or obelisk.toml in project root)
- Persist: selected_runtime, model per runtime, max_concurrent, auto_spawn, poll_interval_secs, agent_timeout_secs (when implemented)
- Auto-save on change (debounced) or on graceful exit
- Use TOML format for consistency with Cargo.toml in this Rust project
- Fall back to hardcoded defaults if config file doesn't exist
- Log config load/save events to the event log

**Example config format:**
```toml
[orchestrator]
runtime = "claude"
max_concurrent = 5
auto_spawn = true
poll_interval_secs = 15

[models]
claude = "claude-opus-4-6"
codex = "gpt-5.4"
copilot = "claude-sonnet-4"
```

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (App::new, add save/load methods), Cargo.toml (add toml dependency)
notes: COMPLETED: Added toml dependency to Cargo.toml. Added ObeliskConfig/OrchestratorConfig/ModelsConfig serde structs. App::new() loads obelisk.toml on startup applying runtime, model, max_concurrent, auto_spawn, poll_interval_secs. Falls back to hardcoded defaults if file missing. App::save_config() saves all settings to obelisk.toml and logs event. save_config() called on graceful exit in main.rs before kill_all_agents. Logs config load/save events to event log. DECISIONS: Save on graceful exit (not debounced) as simplest correct approach.
title: Persist obelisk settings to config file

close_reason: Completed: Added SortMode enum (Priority/Type/Age/Name), sort_ready_tasks(), cycle_sort_mode(), cycle_type_filter(), filtered_tasks() to App. Tasks sorted by priority on every poll. 'f' key cycles sort, 'F' key cycles type filter (ReadyQueue focus). Queue panel title shows active sort/filter state. Auto-spawn and navigation respect filtered view.
description:
The ready queue displays tasks in whatever order 'bd ready --json' returns them. There is no ability to sort by priority, type, or age. In auto-spawn mode, the first task in the list is always picked (app.rs:506), regardless of priority. A P0 critical bug could sit below a P3 chore and never get spawned first.

**Current behavior:**
- on_poll_result (app.rs:334) stores tasks in the order received from bd
- get_auto_spawn_info (app.rs:499) always selects index 0
- No sort controls or filter options in the UI
- render_ready_queue (ui.rs) renders items in list order

**Expected behavior:**
- Sort ready tasks by priority (P0 first) by default, with secondary sort by creation time
- Add a keybinding (e.g., 'f' on dashboard with focus on ReadyQueue) to cycle sort modes: Priority, Type, Age, Name
- Add filter capability: toggle to show only specific types (bug, feature, task, chore) or priority ranges
- Show active sort/filter state in the queue panel title (e.g., "READY QUEUE [sort: priority] [filter: bug,feature]")
- Auto-spawn should always pick the highest-priority task, not just the first in list

**Implementation approach:**
- Add sort_mode: SortMode enum (Priority, Type, Age, Name) to App
- Add type_filter: HashSet<String> and priority_filter: Option<Range<i32>> to App
- Sort tasks in on_poll_result or in a separate sort_ready_tasks() method called after poll
- Apply filters in the render function or maintain a filtered_tasks view
- Update get_auto_spawn_info to respect sorting

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (sorting/filtering logic, App state), src/types.rs (SortMode enum), src/main.rs (filter/sort keybindings), src/ui.rs (sort/filter indicators in panel title)
title: Priority-based sorting and filtering for ready queue

description:
## Summary

When an agent completes successfully, automatically create a GitHub PR from its worktree branch. Close the loop from issue → agent → code → PR without manual intervention.

## Motivation

Right now a completed agent leaves a worktree branch sitting there. Someone still has to manually push and create the PR. That's the bottleneck — the whole point of autonomous agents is removing human toil from the pipeline. Auto-PR creation makes obelisk truly end-to-end.

## Proposed Behavior

- On agent completion (exit 0), optionally trigger PR creation
- Run `gh pr create` from the worktree with auto-generated title and body
- PR body includes: issue ID, agent runtime/model, duration, token usage
- Link back to beads issue (e.g., "Closes obelisk-xyz")
- Configurable: auto-PR on/off, draft vs ready, reviewers
- Event logged: "PR #123 created for AGENT-02 (obelisk-xyz)"
- Dashboard shows PR link in agent detail diagnostics

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Auto PR creation on successful agent completion
- [ ] PR title derived from issue title
- [ ] PR body with agent metadata and issue linkage
- [ ] Toggle for auto-PR (on/off, draft mode)
- [ ] Event log entry with PR URL
- [ ] PR link visible in agent detail
title: Auto-create PR from completed agent worktree

close_reason: Completed: '/' opens inline search bar in AgentDetail observe mode, real-time case-insensitive highlighting (yellow=current, amber=others), n/N navigation, match counter (3/17), Esc to close, search keys in keybindings bar and help overlay
description:
In agent detail view, there is no way to search for text within the agent's terminal output. For long-running agents that produce thousands of lines of output, finding a specific error message, file path, or keyword requires tedious manual scrolling with Page Up/Down.

**Current behavior:**
- Agent output is stored in a vt100::Parser with 10,000 lines of scrollback (app.rs:114)
- Navigation is limited to Up/Down (line by line), Page Up/Down (20 lines), Home/End
- No search, no find, no text filtering

**Expected behavior:**
- Add '/' key in agent detail view (observe mode) to enter search mode
- Show a search input bar at the bottom of the agent output area
- Highlight all matches in the visible area (use a distinct background color, e.g., yellow on dark)
- 'n' / 'N' to jump to next/previous match
- Esc to exit search mode and return to normal observation
- Search should scan the full vt100 scrollback contents, not just the visible screen
- Display match count and current position (e.g., "3/17 matches")
- The vt100 crate's screen.contents() method provides the full text for searching

**Implementation approach:**
- Add search state to App: search_active (bool), search_query (String), search_matches (Vec<(row, col)>), search_current_idx (usize)
- When query changes, scan screen.contents() for all match positions
- Scroll to first match automatically
- Render highlighted matches by checking cell coordinates against match ranges in render_vt100_screen()

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (search state fields, search methods), src/main.rs (handle_key for '/' and search input), src/ui.rs (search bar, match highlighting in render_vt100_screen)
title: Search within agent PTY output

close_reason: Completed: OS desktop notifications (notify-rust) for agent completion/failure with bell fallback, P0/P1 task arrival notifications, notifications_enabled toggle via 'n' key, NOTIFY status in dashboard info bar, help overlay entry
description:
Obelisk is a TUI app that requires the terminal to be focused to see status changes. When running long agent sessions, users often switch to other applications. They have no way to know when an agent completes, fails, or when new high-priority tasks arrive without switching back to the terminal.

**Current behavior:**
- Agent completion/failure is only visible in the TUI (event log + agent list status change)
- The alert_message system (app.rs:221) shows a temporary banner but only within the TUI
- No OS-level notifications, no sound, no external integrations

**Expected behavior:**
- Send OS desktop notifications (toast/banner) for key events:
  - Agent completed successfully
  - Agent failed
  - New P0/P1 tasks detected in ready queue
  - Agent timeout triggered
- Use the 'notify-rust' crate for cross-platform desktop notifications (Windows, macOS, Linux)
- Make notifications configurable: enable/disable per event type
- Optional: terminal bell (\x07) as a lightweight fallback for terminals that support it
- Notification should include: event type, agent ID, task ID, elapsed time

**Implementation approach:**
- Add notify-rust to Cargo.toml dependencies
- Create a notify module or function: send_notification(title: &str, body: &str)
- Call from on_agent_exited, on_poll_result (for P0/P1 tasks), on timeout
- Add notification_enabled: bool to App (persisted in config file when that feature lands)
- Add 'n' keybinding to toggle notifications on/off

**Affected files:** Cargo.toml (notify-rust dep), src/app.rs (notification calls, config), src/main.rs (keybinding), new src/notify.rs or inline in app.rs
title: Desktop notifications for agent completion and critical events

description:
## Summary

Auto-detect small terminal dimensions and switch to a compact layout that drops non-essential panels. Obelisk should be usable in a 80x24 terminal, not just a maximized window.

## Proposed Behavior

- Below certain thresholds, progressively hide panels:
  - < 120 cols: collapse diagnostics sidebar in agent detail
  - < 100 cols: hide throughput sparkline, merge event log into single line
  - < 40 rows: hide detail preview panel, reduce status bar to one line
- Panels reappear when terminal is resized larger
- Core functionality (ready queue, agent list, PTY output) always visible
- No manual toggle needed — purely responsive

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Layout adapts to terminal dimensions
- [ ] Progressive panel hiding at defined breakpoints
- [ ] Panels restore on resize up
- [ ] Core views remain functional at 80x24
title: Compact mode for small terminals

close_reason: Fixed: k key handler immediately killed agents without confirmation. Added confirm_kill_agent_id state, intercept dialog with y/Enter to confirm, n/Esc to cancel. Dialog shows AGENT-XX and issue ID, styled consistently with help overlay.
description:
## Summary

Pressing `k` in agent detail immediately kills the agent with no confirmation. This is a destructive action — the agent's work may be mid-flight, worktree changes could be lost, and the issue may get stuck in a bad state. Should always prompt the user before proceeding.

## Current Behavior

Press `k` → agent is immediately SIGTERM'd and worktree cleanup triggered. No confirmation, no undo.

## Expected Behavior

Press `k` → confirmation dialog appears: "Kill AGENT-02 (obelisk-xyz)? This will terminate the process and clean up the worktree. [y/N]"
- `y` or `Enter` → proceed with kill
- `n` or `Esc` → cancel, return to agent detail
- Dialog styled consistently with existing quit confirmation dialog (obelisk-2uz)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Confirmation dialog shown on `k` press before killing
- [ ] Dialog shows agent ID and issue ID for clarity
- [ ] `y` confirms kill, `n`/`Esc` cancels
- [ ] Consistent styling with existing confirmation dialogs
notes: ROOT CAUSE: k key handler in main.rs immediately called kill_agent() with no user confirmation. FIX: Added confirm_kill_agent_id state field to App, intercept y/Enter/n/Esc when confirmation is pending, render a styled popup dialog showing agent ID and issue ID. REGRESSION TEST: no test infra for TUI.
title: Require confirmation dialog before killing an agent

description:
## Summary

Add the ability to filter the event log by category (SYSTEM, INCOMING, DEPLOY, COMPLETE, ALERT, POLL). When you're debugging poll failures you don't want to scroll past 50 COMPLETE events.

## Proposed Behavior

- Keybinding (e.g., f) in Event Log view cycles through category filters
- Filter modes: ALL → SYSTEM → INCOMING → COMPLETE → ALERT → POLL → ALL
- Active filter shown in panel header: "EVENT LOG [ALERT]"
- Filtered count vs total: "EVENT LOG [3/47] [ALERT]"

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Category filter cycling via keybinding
- [ ] Filter indicator in header
- [ ] Filtered/total count display
title: Event log category filtering

description:
## Summary

Keybinding (e.g., y) to yank the selected issue ID, agent ID, or worktree path to the system clipboard. Small convenience that saves alt-tabbing to copy an ID for use in another terminal.

## Proposed Behavior

- In ready queue: y copies issue ID (e.g., "obelisk-4li")
- In agent list: y copies agent label + issue ID (e.g., "AGENT-02 obelisk-4li")
- In agent detail: y copies worktree path
- Brief flash message: "Copied: obelisk-4li"
- Uses crossterm or OS clipboard integration

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] y keybinding copies context-appropriate data
- [ ] Works in ready queue, agent list, and agent detail
- [ ] Flash confirmation message
title: Copy issue ID or agent info to clipboard

description: Add ability to export agent session logs to a file for debugging. Should capture both raw PTY output and parsed screen content.
title: Implement log export to file

description:
## Summary

Add a panel (toggleable in Dashboard or as part of a new view) that lists all active git worktrees with their branch name, associated issue, status, and age. Quick visibility into what's on disk.

## Proposed Behavior

- List all worktrees from `git worktree list --porcelain`
- For each: path, branch, linked issue ID (parsed from worktree-{id} naming), age
- Status indicator: active (agent running on it), idle (no agent), orphaned (no matching agent or issue)
- Orphaned worktrees highlighted in red with cleanup hint
- Sortable by age or status
- Accessible via keybinding from Dashboard (e.g., `w`)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Worktree list with branch, issue linkage, and age
- [ ] Status classification (active/idle/orphaned)
- [ ] Color-coded orphan highlighting
- [ ] Accessible via keybinding
- [ ] Refreshes on poll cycle
title: Worktree overview panel

description:
## Summary

Comprehensive codebase audit after all current in-flight features land. Review the entire obelisk codebase for architectural coherence, code quality, performance, error handling, and technical debt accumulated from rapid parallel development.

## Motivation

Obelisk has been built at high velocity with many features landing in parallel via autonomous agents. Each feature works individually, but the cumulative effect on codebase quality needs a human-in-the-loop review. This is the checkpoint before the next wave of features — make sure the foundation is solid before building higher.

## Scope

### Architecture Review
- Module boundaries: are app.rs (1400+ lines) and ui.rs (1700+ lines) getting too large? Consider splitting
- State management: is AppState growing unwieldy? Look for coherent sub-states
- Event flow: are there race conditions or timing issues in the async event loop?
- Type design: are the enums and structs well-factored?

### Code Quality
- Dead code elimination — features that got superseded or partially implemented
- Consistency audit — naming conventions, error handling patterns, comment quality
- Duplication — any copy-paste that should be extracted
- Unsafe patterns — any panics, unwraps on user data, or unchecked indexing

### Performance
- PTY reader throughput — are we keeping up with fast output?
- UI render performance — any jank with many agents?
- Memory — are we leaking PTY buffers or output history?
- Poll efficiency — are we doing unnecessary work on each tick?

### Error Handling
- Are all external command failures (bd, git, gh) handled gracefully?
- What happens when the terminal is too small?
- What happens when disk is full during session persistence?

### Testing
- Identify critical paths that need tests
- Integration test strategy for PTY spawning
- UI snapshot testing feasibility

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Architecture review document with findings and recommendations
- [ ] All identified dead code removed
- [ ] Critical error handling gaps fixed
- [ ] Performance bottlenecks identified and filed as issues
- [ ] Module split plan if warranted (filed as follow-up issues)
- [ ] Test coverage plan filed as follow-up issue
title: Codebase audit and quality hardening pass

close_reason: Implemented quit confirmation dialog: shows modal with active agent details when Ctrl+C or q is pressed with running agents. Y/Enter confirms, N/Esc cancels.
description:
When the user presses Ctrl+C or 'q' on the dashboard, obelisk immediately begins shutdown and kills all active agents (app.rs kill_all_agents). There is no confirmation prompt. This is dangerous: a user might accidentally press Ctrl+C or 'q' while agents are mid-implementation, causing them to be killed abruptly. This can leave git worktrees in an inconsistent state, partially committed code, and unclosed beads issues.

**Current behavior (main.rs:236-239, 254-259):**
- Ctrl+C: immediately sets app.should_quit = true
- 'q' on dashboard: immediately sets app.should_quit = true
- Main loop exits → kill_all_agents() → terminal cleanup → process exit
- No warning, no confirmation, no chance to cancel

**Expected behavior:**
- If any agents are in Starting or Running status when quit is triggered:
  - Show a centered modal dialog: "N agent(s) still running. Quit and kill all? [Y/n]"
  - 'y' or Enter confirms → proceed with kill_all_agents + exit
  - 'n' or Esc cancels → return to normal mode, do not quit
  - Show the list of running agent IDs and their tasks in the dialog for context
- If no agents are active, quit immediately without confirmation (current behavior)
- The dialog should be rendered as a modal overlay similar to the existing help overlay (show_help in app.rs:236)
- Add a quit_confirm state to App that controls whether the dialog is visible

**Implementation approach:**
- Add show_quit_confirm: bool to App struct
- In handle_key (main.rs), when quit is triggered and active_agent_count() > 0, set show_quit_confirm = true instead of should_quit = true
- Add key handling for the confirm dialog: 'y'/Enter → should_quit = true, 'n'/Esc → show_quit_confirm = false
- In ui.rs, add render_quit_confirm_dialog() similar to render_help_overlay()
- The dialog should list active agent unit numbers and their task IDs

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (show_quit_confirm field), src/main.rs (handle_key quit logic, confirm dialog key handling), src/ui.rs (render_quit_confirm_dialog)
title: Confirmation dialog on quit with active agents

description:
## Summary

Add a compact horizontal stacked bar to the Dashboard showing the distribution of open issues by priority (P0-P4) and/or type (bug/feature/task/chore). At a glance, see the shape of your backlog.

## Proposed Behavior

- Horizontal bar chart using block characters (▓░ etc.)
- Segments colored by priority: P0=red, P1=orange, P2=yellow, P3=blue, P4=dim
- Shows counts per segment on hover or as inline labels: "P0:2 P1:5 P2:8 P3:3"
- Toggle between priority view and type view (bug/feature/task breakdown)
- Fits in a single row — minimal vertical space
- Updates on each poll cycle as issues are created/closed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Stacked bar rendering with priority-colored segments
- [ ] Inline count labels
- [ ] Toggle between priority and type breakdown
- [ ] Single-row compact layout
- [ ] Updates on poll refresh
title: Priority and type distribution bar in dashboard

description:
## Summary

New TUI view (e.g., key 5) that renders the beads issue dependency tree as an ASCII graph. See what blocks what, what's close to unblocking, and where the critical path is — all without leaving obelisk.

## Proposed Behavior

- Render output of `bd dep tree` as a navigable, color-coded tree view
- Nodes colored by status: green=done, blue=in-progress, dim=open, red=blocked
- Highlight the critical path (longest chain of unresolved deps)
- Arrow keys to navigate/expand/collapse subtrees
- Enter on a node to jump to its detail or show inline preview
- Auto-refreshes on poll cycle

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New view accessible via keybinding
- [ ] Tree rendered from `bd dep tree` or `bd dep list` data
- [ ] Color-coded nodes by status
- [ ] Navigable with keyboard
- [ ] Collapsible subtrees
title: Dependency graph visualization view

close_reason: Completed: agent_timeout_secs field (default 1800s), timeout watchdog in on_tick() with 80% warning, kill_agent_timeout() method, LogCategory::Timeout, TIMEOUT countdown in diagnostics panel, 't' keybinding cycling 5m/15m/30m/1h/off presets, help overlay and keybindings bar updated. Build succeeds.
description:
There is no mechanism to detect or kill hung agents. If an agent process hangs indefinitely (e.g., waiting for input it will never receive, network timeout, infinite loop), it will consume a concurrency slot forever and the user must manually navigate to agent detail and press 'k'.

**Current behavior:**
- Agent elapsed time is tracked (app.rs:312-314) but never acted upon
- No timeout threshold exists — agents can run for hours without intervention
- Active agent count gates new spawns via max_concurrent, so hung agents block new work
- Auto-spawn mode is especially vulnerable: hung agents fill all slots, stalling the queue

**Expected behavior:**
- Add a configurable agent_timeout_secs field to App (default: 1800 = 30 minutes)
- In on_tick(), check each active agent's elapsed_secs against the timeout
- When exceeded: kill the agent (SIGTERM), set status to Failed, log a timeout alert
- Add keybinding (e.g., 't' on dashboard) to adjust timeout, or use '+'/'-' style controls
- Show timeout countdown in agent detail diagnostics panel (e.g., "timeout in 12:34")
- Consider a warning alert at 80% of timeout threshold
- Distinguish timeout failures from normal failures in the event log (new LogCategory::Timeout or suffix in message)

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (App struct, on_tick, kill_agent), src/types.rs (LogCategory), src/ui.rs (diagnostics panel, info bar), src/main.rs (keybinding for timeout adjustment)
notes: COMPLETED: agent_timeout_secs field (default 1800s) in App struct; on_tick() watchdog checks elapsed vs timeout; kill_agent_timeout() with LogCategory::Timeout; 80% warning threshold; timeout countdown in diagnostics panel; 't' keybinding cycles presets (5m/15m/30m/1h/disabled); help overlay updated; log_category_color for Timeout. DECISIONS: presets chosen as common useful values; warn_needed excludes already-timed-out agents to avoid double-log.
title: Agent timeout watchdog for hung processes

close_reason: Completed: PTY output completion detection for ClaudeCode agents — scans for status:closed in bd JSON output, sends /exit to PTY, force-kills after 10s timeout, configurable auto_exit_on_completion setting with z toggle keybinding
description:
Agent processes spawned in PTY mode run indefinitely even after completing their beads workflow. Claude Code in particular stays in interactive mode after finishing its initial prompt (no --print flag is used, per runtime.rs:39-46). When the agent closes the beads issue and completes Phase 7, the session remains open and the agent idles, consuming a concurrency slot and resources. The user must manually press 'k' to kill each completed agent.

**Root cause (runtime.rs:36-46):**
Claude Code is spawned WITHOUT --print, keeping its interactive REPL open:
```rust
Runtime::ClaudeCode => {
    let mut cmd = CommandBuilder::new("claude");
    cmd.arg(user_prompt);
    // No --print → stays interactive after first response
```

Codex and Copilot use one-shot exec modes and exit naturally, but Claude Code (the primary runtime) does not.

**Current behavior:**
- Claude Code agent finishes all phases, closes the beads issue, but the process stays alive in its REPL
- Agent status remains "Running ▶" indefinitely
- The concurrency slot is wasted — no new tasks can take it
- Auto-spawn mode cannot reclaim the slot
- User must manually detect completion by reading PTY output and pressing 'k'

**Expected behavior:**
- Parse incoming PTY data (on_agent_pty_data in app.rs:757) for completion markers
- Detect when the agent has finished its workflow by looking for patterns like:
  - 'bd close {task_id}' with success output
  - 'status: closed' in bd show output
  - The agent explicitly saying it's done (e.g., "Issue completed", "Work complete")
- When completion is detected, automatically send '/exit' or 'exit' to the Claude Code PTY to gracefully close the session
- If the process doesn't exit within 10 seconds after the exit command, send SIGTERM
- Log the auto-completion event: "AGENT-XX auto-completed: detected issue closure"
- Only apply this behavior to Claude Code runtime (Codex/Copilot already exit naturally)

**Implementation approach:**
- Add a completion_detected: bool field to AgentInstance (or phase tracking if obelisk-9t1 lands first)
- In on_agent_pty_data, convert incoming bytes to string and scan for completion markers
- Use a simple state machine: detect "bd close" → wait for success confirmation → send exit command
- Consider also polling 'bd show {task_id} --json' periodically for the active agent to check if status is closed (belt-and-suspenders)
- Add a configurable auto_exit_on_completion: bool setting (default true)

**Alternative approach:**
- Switch to using --print flag for Claude Code, making it one-shot. However, this loses the ability to attach interactively mid-session, which is a core feature. The PTY parsing approach preserves interactivity while adding auto-exit.

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (on_agent_pty_data completion detection, auto-exit logic), src/types.rs (AgentInstance.completion_detected), src/runtime.rs (optional: add exit timeout constant)
notes: COMPLETED: Added completion_detected/exit_sent_at/completion_buf fields to AgentInstance; check_completion_in_pty_data scans PTY output for status:closed markers; on_tick enforces 10s force-kill timeout via force_complete_agent; auto_exit_on_completion bool on App (default true); 'z' key toggles it; all 7 tests pass. DECISIONS: Only applies to ClaudeCode runtime. Marks as Completed not Failed on timeout.
title: Auto-detect agent session completion via PTY output parsing

close_reason: Not a real bug - on_agent_exited already sets Completed/Failed correctly
title: Fix agent status not updating after exit

close_reason: Completed: Added dismiss_selected_agent() ('x') and dismiss_all_finished() ('X') keybindings. Active agents are protected. Dismissed agents removed from self.agents and self.pty_states freeing vt100 parser memory. Selection state adjusted after removal. total_completed/total_failed counters unaffected. Status bar and help overlay updated with new keybindings.
description:
Completed and failed agents remain in the agent list indefinitely. Over a long session with many tasks, the list becomes cluttered with dead agents, making it harder to find active ones. There is no way to dismiss, archive, or clear finished agents.

**Current behavior:**
- Agents are pushed to self.agents (app.rs:466) and never removed
- All agents (active + dead) render in the agent list panel
- The agent list has no filtering or grouping by status
- Navigation wraps through all agents including completed/failed ones

**Expected behavior:**
- Add a keybinding (e.g., 'x' or Delete) to dismiss a selected completed/failed agent from the list
- Add 'X' or Ctrl+x to dismiss ALL completed/failed agents at once
- Dismissed agents should be removed from self.agents and self.pty_states (free memory from vt100 parser)
- Do NOT allow dismissing active (Starting/Running) agents — only completed/failed
- Consider auto-dismiss option: automatically remove agents N minutes after completion
- Optionally group agents in the list: active first, then completed, then failed (visual separator)
- Total completed/failed counters should NOT be affected by dismissal (they're already tracked separately)

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (dismiss methods, optional auto-dismiss in on_tick), src/main.rs (keybinding), src/ui.rs (optional grouping/separator in render_agents_panel)
title: Dismiss completed/failed agents from agent list

close_reason: Completed: / keybinding on Dashboard opens jump bar with live fuzzy matching across ready queue and agents. Enter jumps to matched entry, Esc dismisses. Help overlay updated.
description:
## Summary

Press a keybinding (e.g., g or /) to open a mini input prompt, type an issue ID, and jump directly to it in the ready queue or agent list. Faster than scrolling through a long list.

## Proposed Behavior

- Keybinding opens a one-line input field at the bottom of the screen
- Type partial or full issue ID (e.g., "4li")
- Fuzzy match against ready queue and agent list
- Enter to jump, Esc to cancel
- If match is in ready queue, select it there; if it's an agent, switch focus and select

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Input prompt on keybinding
- [ ] Fuzzy match across ready queue and agent list
- [ ] Jump to matched entry
- [ ] Esc to dismiss
title: Quick jump to issue by ID
Add color theme configuration

close_reason: Completed: velocity sparkline widget on dashboard showing per-session issue completion rate with configurable window (default 24), INFO-colored sparkline positioned between throughput and events panels, real-time updates as issues close, peak rate annotation
description:
## Summary

Add a small burndown/velocity sparkline to the Dashboard showing issues completed over time. A quick visual pulse of how fast the backlog is shrinking.

## Proposed Behavior

- Sparkline widget (similar to existing throughput sparkline) showing completed issues per hour or per session
- Data sourced from session history (obelisk_sessions.jsonl) + current session
- Shows last 24 data points (configurable window)
- Positioned near the existing throughput sparkline or in the status gauge area
- Label: "VELOCITY" with peak rate annotation
- Hoverable or selectable to show exact counts per bucket

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sparkline rendered from historical completion data
- [ ] Updates in real-time as issues close during session
- [ ] Configurable time window
- [ ] Positioned cleanly in dashboard layout
notes: COMPLETED: velocity sparkline widget with per-session completion data, configurable window via obelisk.toml, INFO-colored sparkline positioned between throughput and events in dashboard bottom section. Real-time updates via total_completed. DECISIONS: used per-session granularity (not per-hour) since session records provide natural data points.
title: Issue burndown and velocity sparkline

description:
## Summary

Implement an automated merge queue that processes completed agent PRs in priority order. After an agent's PR passes CI checks, auto-merge it, rebase the next PR, and continue down the queue.

## Motivation

With auto-PR creation, you'll have a stream of PRs from agents. Manually reviewing and merging each one defeats the purpose of automation. A merge queue handles the sequencing — respecting priority order, waiting for CI, handling rebase conflicts — while you focus on the work that actually needs a human.

## Proposed Behavior

- New panel or section in Dashboard showing merge queue state
- PRs enter queue when agent completes and PR is created
- Queue ordered by issue priority (P0 first)
- Each PR: wait for CI green → auto-merge → rebase next PR in queue
- Conflict detection: if rebase fails, flag for manual intervention
- Configurable: require approval before merge, auto-merge only drafts→ready, etc.
- Event log: "PR #123 merged (obelisk-xyz, P1)" / "PR #124 rebase conflict — manual intervention needed"

## Dependencies

Depends on: Auto-create PR from completed agent worktree

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Merge queue visualization in dashboard
- [ ] Priority-ordered queue processing
- [ ] CI status check before merge
- [ ] Auto-rebase of queued PRs
- [ ] Conflict detection and alerting
- [ ] Configurable merge policies
title: Merge queue for completed agent PRs

description:
## Summary

The auto-exit/session completion detection for Claude agents is unreliable and causes more problems than it solves. It should be removed entirely. Let Claude exit naturally when it finishes its work rather than trying to detect completion from PTY output and force an exit.

## Problem

The auto-detect agent session completion feature (obelisk-4li) parses PTY output to guess when Claude is done, but it's flakey — false positives kill agents mid-work, false negatives leave it doing nothing. Combined with obelisk-kg8 (exit code 0 reported as failed), this creates a bad experience where agents appear to fail when they actually completed, or get terminated before they're actually done.

## Proposed Fix

- Remove the auto-exit detection logic for Claude runtime
- Let the Claude CLI handle its own lifecycle — it exits on its own when done
- Clean up any related flags (auto_exit_on_completion) and PTY output parsing for session end detection
- Keep the manual kill (k) as the only way to force-terminate an agent

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Auto-exit detection removed for Claude agents
- [ ] Claude agents run until the CLI exits on its own
- [ ] Related flags and detection code cleaned up
- [ ] Manual kill still works as before
title: Remove auto-exit detection for Claude agents

close_reason: Completed: Added SessionRecord/SessionAgent types, session persistence to .beads/obelisk_sessions.jsonl on graceful exit, last-session startup summary in event log, aggregate_stats() for all-time stats, and History view (tab 4) showing all-time statistics panel plus scrollable session list.
description:
All session statistics (total_completed, total_failed, event_log) are in-memory only and lost on every restart. There is no way to review historical agent performance, identify recurring failures, or track throughput trends across sessions.

**Current behavior:**
- total_completed, total_failed are u32 counters in App (app.rs:204-205), reset to 0 on startup
- event_log is a VecDeque<LogEntry> capped at 500 entries (app.rs:290), lost on exit
- throughput_history is a 60-frame rolling window (app.rs:217), no persistence
- No session ID, no start/end timestamps for the session itself

**Expected behavior:**
- On graceful exit, append session summary to a persistent log file (e.g., .beads/obelisk_sessions.jsonl)
- Session record should include: session_id, start_time, end_time, total_completed, total_failed, tasks_processed (list of task IDs + outcomes), runtime distribution, avg_agent_duration
- On startup, optionally display a brief summary of the last session
- Add a view or panel showing aggregate stats across all sessions (total tasks completed all-time, success rate, average completion time)
- Consider also persisting the event log to a rotating log file for post-mortem analysis

**Session record schema (JSONL):**
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "started_at": "2026-03-12T10:00:00Z",
  "ended_at": "2026-03-12T14:30:00Z",
  "total_completed": 12,
  "total_failed": 2,
  "agents": [
    {"task_id": "obelisk-abc", "runtime": "CLAUDE", "model": "claude-opus-4-6", "elapsed_secs": 340, "status": "Completed"}
  ]
}
```

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (session tracking, save on exit), src/main.rs (load on startup, save before exit), src/types.rs (SessionRecord struct)
title: Session history and aggregate statistics persistence

close_reason: Completed: token usage parsing from PTY output via regex, per-agent cost estimation with configurable model pricing table, cost/token display in agent diagnostics and status bar, session cost persistence and aggregate history view, optional cost threshold alerting
description:
## Summary

Track and display API token usage and estimated cost per agent, per session, and in aggregate. Surface this in the agent detail diagnostics panel and session history.

## Motivation

Running multiple agents burns tokens. Right now there's zero visibility into spend. You could be hemorrhaging money on a stuck agent and not know it. Cost awareness changes how you use the tool — you might pick haiku for simple bugs and opus for complex features, or kill an agent that's spinning.

## Proposed Behavior

- Parse token usage from agent CLI output (Claude CLI prints usage stats)
- Track input/output tokens per agent
- Calculate estimated cost based on model pricing (configurable rates)
- Display in Agent Detail diagnostics: tokens in/out, estimated cost
- Display in Dashboard: session total cost
- Display in History view: cost per session, all-time spend
- Cost threshold alerts (configurable) — flash warning when agent exceeds budget

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Token usage parsed from agent output (regex on Claude CLI format)
- [ ] Per-agent cost displayed in diagnostics panel
- [ ] Session total cost in dashboard status bar
- [ ] Historical cost in session history
- [ ] Configurable model pricing table
- [ ] Optional cost threshold warning
title: Agent cost and token usage tracking

description:
## Summary

Automatically select the optimal runtime and model based on issue characteristics — type, priority, estimated complexity, and labels. Simple bugs get fast cheap models, complex features get the heavy hitters.

## Motivation

Right now model selection is manual (press 'm' to cycle). When auto-spawn is on, everything gets the same model. That's wasteful — you're paying opus prices for a one-line typo fix, or getting haiku-quality output on an architectural feature. Smart routing optimizes both cost and quality.

## Proposed Behavior

- Define routing rules in config: map issue attributes to runtime/model
- Example rules:
  - `bug + P3/P4` → haiku (fast, cheap)
  - `feature + P0/P1` → opus (thorough)
  - `chore` → codex (fast batch work)
  - Label `complex` → opus, label `trivial` → haiku
- Rules evaluated in priority order, first match wins
- Fallback to current selected model if no rules match
- Override per-spawn still possible (manual 'm' selection)
- Display chosen model reasoning in event log

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Routing rules configurable (config file or in-app)
- [ ] Rules match on type, priority, labels, estimate
- [ ] Auto-spawn uses routing rules
- [ ] Manual override still works
- [ ] Event log shows routing decision
- [ ] Fallback behavior when no rules match
title: Smart model routing based on issue characteristics

close_reason: Completed: Added task detail preview panel as a bottom split of the ready queue left column. Displays full title, word-wrapped description, priority, type, labels, and assignee. Shows '(no description)' when empty. Updates live on navigation.
description:
The dashboard ready queue shows only a one-line summary per task: priority badge, type, ID, and truncated title. There is no way to view the task's full description, acceptance criteria, labels, or assignee without leaving obelisk and running `bd show <id>` in a separate terminal.

**Current behavior:**
- Ready queue items rendered as single-line ListItems in ui.rs render_ready_queue()
- BeadTask struct (types.rs:7-21) already deserializes description, labels, and assignee from `bd ready --json`, but these fields are never displayed
- No preview or detail panel exists on the dashboard

**Expected behavior:**
- When a task is selected in the ready queue (highlighted), show a detail preview panel below the queue or as a popup
- Display: full title, description (with word wrap), priority, type, labels, assignee
- Could be a bottom split of the left panel, or a modal popup toggled with a key (e.g., 'd' for detail)
- Preview should update as the user navigates up/down through the queue
- If description is empty, show "(no description)" placeholder

**Affected files:** src/ui.rs (new render function, layout adjustment), src/main.rs (keybinding if modal), src/app.rs (state for preview toggle if modal)
notes: COMPLETED: Added render_task_preview() function in ui.rs that shows selected task's full title, description (word-wrapped via Paragraph::wrap), priority, type, labels, assignee below the ready queue. Left column in render_dashboard() now splits vertically: queue list (Min(4)) + detail preview (Length(9)). Shows '(no description)' placeholder when empty. Updates automatically on j/k/↑↓ navigation. DECISIONS: Used always-visible bottom split (not a toggle) for simplicity and immediate visibility.
title: Task detail preview panel in dashboard

close_reason: Completed: startup orphan scan with alerts, 'c' key bulk cleanup, auto-cleanup on agent kill, worktree status in agent diagnostics, WorktreeOrphans/WorktreeCleaned events, scan_agent_worktrees() and cleanup_worktree() in runtime.rs
description:
The agent prompt template (app.rs Phase 2) instructs agents to create git worktrees at ../worktree-{id}. If an agent fails mid-execution (crash, timeout, kill), the worktree and its branch remain on disk and in git's worktree registry. Over time, orphaned worktrees accumulate, consuming disk space and cluttering 'git worktree list' output.

**Current behavior:**
- Agents create worktrees but obelisk has no awareness of them
- kill_agent() (app.rs:680) only sends SIGTERM/taskkill — no worktree cleanup
- No UI surface shows worktree status or allows cleanup
- 'git worktree list' is the only way to discover orphans (outside obelisk)

**Expected behavior:**
- On agent failure/kill, attempt automatic cleanup: 'git worktree remove ../worktree-{task_id} --force'
- Add a cleanup command/keybinding (e.g., 'c' on dashboard) that scans for orphaned worktrees
- Show worktree status in agent detail diagnostics: "Worktree: ../worktree-obelisk-abc (active)" or "(cleaned up)"
- On obelisk startup, run 'git worktree list' and warn if orphan worktrees exist from previous sessions
- Add a bulk cleanup option for all orphaned worktrees

**Implementation approach:**
- Extract task_id from AgentInstance to derive worktree path pattern
- Use tokio::process::Command to run 'git worktree list --porcelain' for detection
- Use 'git worktree remove <path> --force' for cleanup and 'git branch -D <branch>' for the branch
- Add worktree_path field to AgentInstance for tracking
- Log cleanup events to the event log

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (kill_agent cleanup, startup check, worktree tracking), src/types.rs (AgentInstance.worktree_path), src/main.rs (startup scan, keybinding), src/runtime.rs (optional: extract worktree path from agent config)
title: Orphaned worktree detection and cleanup

close_reason: Completed: Added AgentPhase enum (8 phases: Detecting through Done) to types.rs. Added phase field to AgentInstance. Added detect_phase() in app.rs scanning PTY output for phase markers (--claim, git worktree add, --notes, cargo test/check/clippy, --no-ff, bd close). Phase only advances. Added render_phase_indicator() step bar in agent detail header, [P3] compact badge in dashboard agent list, and PHASE row in diagnostics panel. Color-coded: blue=early phases, amber=implementing, green=verify/merge/close.
description:
The agent prompt template defines a 7-phase workflow (Phase 0: Detect conventions → Phase 1: Claim → Phase 2: Worktree → Phase 3: Implement → Phase 4: Verify → Phase 5: Merge → Phase 6: Close → Phase 7: Verify completion), but the UI only shows four coarse statuses: Starting ◐, Running ▶, Completed ✓, Failed ✗. There is no visibility into which phase the agent is currently executing.

**Current behavior:**
- AgentStatus enum (types.rs:70-76) has only 4 variants
- Agent detail diagnostics panel shows status as a single word
- The only way to determine phase is to read the raw PTY output and look for phase markers

**Expected behavior:**
- Parse agent PTY output for phase transition markers (e.g., 'bd update --claim', 'git worktree add', 'bd close')
- Track current phase per agent: Detecting → Claiming → Worktree → Implementing → Verifying → Merging → Closing → Done
- Display phase as a progress bar or step indicator in the agent detail view header
- Show phase in the dashboard agent list as a compact badge (e.g., "P3" for Phase 3: Implementing)
- Color-code phases: early phases (blue), implementation (yellow), verification/merge (green)

**Implementation approach:**
- Add a phase: AgentPhase enum to AgentInstance
- In on_agent_pty_data, scan incoming data for phase-indicating patterns:
  - "bd update.*--claim" → Claiming
  - "git worktree add" → Worktree
  - "bd close" → Closing
  - Test/lint commands → Verifying
- These are heuristic and best-effort — don't block on perfect detection
- Add a render_phase_indicator() function in ui.rs

**Affected files:** src/types.rs (AgentPhase enum), src/app.rs (AgentInstance.phase, on_agent_pty_data phase detection), src/ui.rs (phase indicator rendering in agent detail + dashboard)
notes: COMPLETED: Added AgentPhase enum (Detecting/Claiming/Worktree/Implementing/Verifying/Merging/Closing/Done) to types.rs. Added phase field to AgentInstance. Added detect_phase() heuristic scanner in app.rs that advances phase based on PTY output patterns (--claim, git worktree add, --notes, cargo test/check/clippy, --no-ff, bd close). Phase only advances, never retreats. Added render_phase_indicator() step bar in ui.rs shown in agent detail header. Added phase badge [P3] to dashboard agent list. Added PHASE row to diagnostics panel. All color-coded (blue=early, amber=implementing, green=verify/merge/close). Compiles clean.
title: Agent workflow phase progress indicator

close_reason: Completed: fixed PTY resize artifacts by (1) replacing vt100 parser with fresh instance on resize instead of set_size() reflow, (2) calling terminal.clear() on terminal size change in render loop, (3) removing redundant sync_pty_sizes from Resize event handler
description: When the terminal window is resized while an agent is running, the vt100 screen sometimes shows garbled output until the next full redraw.
notes: COMPLETED: Fixed PTY resize artifacts via three coordinated changes: (1) resize_agent_pty now replaces vt100::Parser with a fresh instance instead of calling set_size() — avoids garbled reflow content during SIGWINCH repaint window; (2) render loop calls terminal.clear() when terminal size changes — forces ratatui full repaint; (3) removed redundant sync_pty_sizes call from Resize event handler since the render loop already handles it. Build passes. Rebased on main after obelisk-obv merge.
title: PTY resize causes rendering artifacts

close_reason: Completed: Mouse support for list selection (click to select in ready queue and agent list), scroll wheel navigation (all views), tab bar click to switch views, split pane click to focus, 'M' toggle. Implemented via LayoutAreas hit-testing struct. No interference with keyboard controls.
description:
## Summary

Enable basic mouse support via crossterm: click to select items in ready queue and agent list, scroll wheel to navigate, click tabs to switch views.

## Proposed Behavior

- Left click on a ready queue item to select it
- Left click on an agent list item to select it
- Scroll wheel to navigate lists (whichever panel the cursor is over)
- Click on tab bar labels (1-4) to switch views
- Click on agent in detail view scrolls output
- Does not replace keyboard — purely additive
- Toggle on/off if some terminal emulators have issues

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Click to select in ready queue and agent list
- [ ] Scroll wheel navigation
- [ ] Click tab bar to switch views
- [ ] No interference with existing keyboard controls
notes: COMPLETED: Mouse support implemented. Click to select in ready queue/agent list, scroll wheel navigation in all views, click tab bar to switch views, 'M' toggle for mouse on/off. DECISIONS: Used LayoutAreas struct for hit-testing rather than coordinate calculations. Mouse enabled by default.
title: Mouse support for list selection and scrolling

close_reason: Completed: Added AppEvent::PollFailed(String) variant; poll health state tracking (last_poll_ok, last_poll_error, consecutive_poll_failures) in App; on_poll_failed handler logs Alert events and shows urgent warning after 3 consecutive failures; on_poll_result clears error state and logs recovery; both background poller and manual 'p' handler send PollFailed on error; process_event dispatches PollFailed; render_poll_error_banner in ui.rs shows persistent red banner with error text, and a 2-line urgent dolt server hint after 3+ failures; poll cycle gauge reflects error state visually.
description:
When the bd CLI fails (e.g., dolt server is down, bd is not in PATH, database corruption), the poller silently swallows the error and sends an empty task list. The user sees an empty ready queue with no indication that polling is broken — indistinguishable from 'no tasks available.'

**Current behavior (main.rs:91-93):**
\`\`\`rust
Err(_e) => {
    let _ = tx_poll.send(AppEvent::PollResult(Vec::new()));
}
\`\`\`
- Poll errors are discarded — no log entry, no alert, no visual cue
- The poll countdown resets normally as if the scan succeeded
- on_poll_result (app.rs:334) logs 'Scan complete: 0 ready' even when the scan actually failed

**Expected behavior:**
- Add a new AppEvent variant: PollFailed(String) with the error message
- In main.rs poller, send PollFailed instead of empty PollResult on error
- In App, track poll health state (last_poll_ok: bool, last_poll_error: Option<String>)
- Display a persistent error banner in the UI when poll is failing (red background, error text)
- Log each failure to the event log with LogCategory::Alert
- After N consecutive failures (e.g., 3), show a more urgent warning suggesting the user check dolt server status
- When polling recovers, clear the error banner and log recovery

**Affected files:** src/types.rs (AppEvent::PollFailed), src/app.rs (poll health state, on_poll_failed handler), src/main.rs (process_event, poller error path), src/ui.rs (error banner rendering)
notes: COMPLETED: Added AppEvent::PollFailed(String), poll health state in App (last_poll_ok, last_poll_error, consecutive_poll_failures), on_poll_failed handler with Alert logging and urgent warning after 3 failures, recovery logging in on_poll_result, PollFailed dispatch in process_event and manual poll handler, render_poll_error_banner in ui.rs (persistent red banner, 2-line urgent hint at 3+ failures), and poll gauge error state display.
title: Visual indicator when bd CLI polling fails

close_reason: Fixed: agent_line_count() was reading vt100 screen rows (capped at terminal height). Added total_lines counter on AgentInstance that increments on each newline received, surviving PTY resizes.
description:
## Summary

The line count displayed in agent diagnostics caps at ~37 (the visible terminal height) instead of tracking total lines of output. It's likely reading the current visible rows from the vt100 parser screen rather than accumulating total lines received.

## Expected Behavior

Line count should increment continuously as the agent produces output — hundreds or thousands of lines for a long-running agent.

## Actual Behavior

Line count plateaus around 37 (or whatever the PTY row count is), suggesting it's reading `parser.screen().size()` or visible row count instead of a running total.

## Proposed Fix

- Maintain a separate `total_lines` counter on the AgentInstance
- Increment on each newline character received in the PTY reader (or count lines from raw byte chunks)
- Display this counter in diagnostics and agent list, not the parser screen dimensions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Line count reflects total output lines, not visible terminal rows
- [ ] Counter increments as new output arrives
- [ ] Displayed correctly in agent detail diagnostics and agent list
notes: ROOT CAUSE: agent_line_count() read vt100 screen rows + scrollback, capping at terminal height. On resize, parser was replaced with a fresh instance, resetting scrollback to zero. FIX: Added total_lines counter on AgentInstance, incremented on each newline in on_agent_pty_data(). agent_line_count() now returns this counter. REGRESSION TEST: no dedicated test (no unit test infrastructure for App), but all 7 existing integration tests pass.
title: Agent line count stuck at terminal height instead of total output lines

close_reason: Completed: removed timeout watchdog logic, kill_agent_timeout method, agent_timeout_secs/timeout_warned_ids state fields, LogCategory::Timeout variant, 't' keybinding, and all timeout UI elements. Manual kill (k) and auto-exit remain as the only force-termination mechanisms.
description:
## Summary

Remove the agent timeout watchdog functionality (obelisk-3df). Agents should run until they finish or are manually killed — no automatic timeout termination.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Timeout watchdog logic removed
- [ ] No automatic agent termination based on elapsed time
- [ ] Related config/state fields cleaned up
- [ ] Manual kill (k) remains the only way to force-terminate
title: Remove agent timeout watchdog

close_reason: Completed: Added AgentStatusFilter enum (All/Running/Failed/Completed/Starting), 'f' keybinding cycles filter when AgentList focused, panel title shows filter badge and filtered/total count, navigation/enter/dismiss operate on filtered view, filter resets on Tab away, help overlay updated
description:
## Summary

Add the ability to filter the agent list panel by agent status. Currently the agent list shows all agents (running, completed, failed, starting) in a flat list. As the number of agents grows, it becomes harder to quickly find agents in a specific state.

## Motivation

When running many agents simultaneously, the agent list can become cluttered. Users often want to:
- See only **failed** agents to investigate errors and retry them
- See only **running** agents to monitor active work
- Hide **completed** agents without permanently dismissing them
- Quickly toggle between filtered and unfiltered views

The existing dismiss feature (x/X keybindings) permanently removes finished agents. A filter provides a non-destructive way to focus on agents of interest.

## Proposed Behavior

- Add a keybinding (e.g., `f` or `F`) to cycle through agent status filters in the agent list panel
- Filter modes: All → Running → Failed → Completed → Starting → All
- Display the active filter in the agent list header (e.g., "AGENTS [▶ RUNNING]")
- Filtering should not affect agent selection state — if the selected agent becomes hidden, selection moves to the nearest visible agent
- The filter state should reset when switching focus away from the agent panel (or optionally persist)

## Agent Statuses

The current `AgentStatus` enum has four variants:
- `Starting` — agent is initializing
- `Running` — agent is actively working  
- `Completed` — agent finished successfully
- `Failed` — agent terminated with an error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Keybinding to cycle through status filters in agent list
- [ ] Visual indicator of active filter in panel header
- [ ] Agent count reflects filtered count (e.g., "AGENTS [2/5] [▶ RUNNING]")
- [ ] Selection gracefully handles filtered-out agents
- [ ] Help overlay updated with new keybinding
title: Filter agent list by status (failed/running/completed)

close_reason: Completed: markdown rendering in task detail preview panel. Supports headers (bold+color), bold/italic text, bullet lists, checkboxes, inline code, and fenced code blocks. Falls back to raw text gracefully.
description:
## Summary

Render basic markdown formatting in the task detail preview panel. Currently issue descriptions display as raw text. Light markdown rendering (headers, bold, lists, code blocks) makes dense issue descriptions scannable.

## Proposed Behavior

- Parse issue description for common markdown elements
- Render: `# headers` as bold+color, `**bold**`, `- lists` with bullets, ``` code blocks ``` with dim background
- No full markdown engine needed — just the common patterns
- Applies to the task detail preview panel in Dashboard and any future issue popups
- Graceful fallback: if parsing fails, show raw text as today

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Headers rendered as bold with accent color
- [ ] Bold/italic text styled appropriately
- [ ] List items rendered with bullet characters
- [ ] Code blocks rendered with distinct style
- [ ] Fallback to raw text on parse failure
title: Markdown-rendered issue description in detail preview

close_reason: Completed: added 'r' keybinding in AgentDetail view to retry failed agents; retry_count field on AgentInstance; max_retries=3 limit with alert when exceeded; RETRIES shown in diagnostics panel with color coding
description:
When an agent fails (exit code != 0), the user has no way to retry the same task. The task ID is permanently added to `claimed_task_ids` in app.rs:465 and filtered out of poll results in app.rs:338, so it never reappears in the ready queue. The agent remains in the list with a ✗ status but cannot be restarted.

**Current behavior:**
- Agent fails → status set to Failed, task ID stays in claimed_task_ids
- Task never reappears in ready queue even on next poll
- User must quit obelisk, manually unclaim the task via `bd update <id> --status open`, then restart

**Expected behavior:**
- Add a keybinding (e.g., 'R' in agent detail view) to retry a failed agent
- Retry should: remove the task ID from claimed_task_ids, create a new AgentInstance with the same BeadTask, spawn a fresh PTY process
- Optionally increment a retry counter on the AgentInstance for visibility
- Consider adding a configurable max_retries limit (default 3) before permanently marking a task as unretryable
- Show retry count in the agent detail diagnostics panel

**Affected files:** src/app.rs (claimed_task_ids, get_spawn_info), src/main.rs (handle_key), src/types.rs (AgentInstance), src/ui.rs (diagnostics panel)
title: Agent retry/restart on failure

close_reason: Completed: Added failure context handoff on retry - extracts last 80 lines of PTY output, exit code, and error patterns (compile errors, test failures, panics, permission denied, merge conflicts) from failed agent; appends context to retry prompt with attempt number; configurable context window via retry_context_lines
description:
## Summary

When retrying a failed agent, capture the failure context (error output, last N lines of PTY, exit code, stack traces) and inject it into the retry agent's prompt so it doesn't repeat the same mistake.

## Motivation

Currently retry (press 'r') spawns a fresh agent with the same prompt — it has zero knowledge of what went wrong before. The agent hits the same wall, fails the same way, burns the same tokens. Context handoff breaks the cycle: "the previous attempt failed because X, avoid Y, try Z instead."

## Proposed Behavior

- On retry, extract failure context from the failed agent:
  - Last 50-100 lines of PTY output
  - Exit code and signal info
  - Any detected error patterns (compilation errors, test failures)
- Append failure context to the retry agent's system prompt
- Format: "PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FAILED: [context]. Avoid the same approach."
- Configurable context window size
- Retry count visible in prompt so agent knows it's attempt #2/3

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Failure context extracted from PTY output on retry
- [ ] Context appended to retry agent prompt
- [ ] Configurable context window (lines to include)
- [ ] Retry attempt number included in prompt
- [ ] Error pattern detection (compile errors, test failures)
title: Agent failure context handoff on retry

close_reason: Completed: per-type agent prompt templates with bug/feature/task/chore/epic workflows, configurable template dir (.obelisk/templates/), variable interpolation ({id},{title},{priority},{description}), hot-reload from disk, built-in defaults via include_str\!, template name in agent diagnostics panel
description:
## Summary

Replace the monolithic hardcoded agent prompt template with configurable per-type templates. Bugs, features, chores, and epics each get a workflow tuned to their nature.

## Motivation

The current 175-line prompt template in app.rs is one-size-fits-all. But a bug fix workflow (reproduce → diagnose → fix → regression test) is fundamentally different from a feature workflow (design → implement → test → document). Custom templates let each issue type get purpose-built instructions.

## Proposed Behavior

- Template files stored in `.obelisk/templates/` or configurable path
- Default templates: `bug.md`, `feature.md`, `task.md`, `chore.md`, `epic.md`
- Templates support variable interpolation: `{id}`, `{title}`, `{priority}`, `{description}`
- Fallback to built-in default if no template file exists for a type
- Hot-reloadable: edit template, next spawn uses updated version
- Template selection shown in agent detail diagnostics

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Template loading from configurable directory
- [ ] Per-type template resolution with fallback
- [ ] Variable interpolation in templates
- [ ] Hot-reload without restart
- [ ] Built-in defaults for all issue types
- [ ] Template name shown in agent diagnostics
notes: COMPLETED: template module with per-type resolution, variable interpolation, built-in defaults for all 5 types, hot-reload from disk, template name in agent diagnostics. DECISIONS: templates compiled into binary via include_str! for built-in defaults; disk templates in .obelisk/templates/ take precedence.
title: Agent prompt templates per issue type

description:
## Summary

When a Claude Code agent finishes its task normally and exits, obelisk marks it as Failed instead of Completed. The Claude CLI exits cleanly (exit code 0) after completing work, but obelisk misinterprets the process termination.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Spawn an agent on an issue using Claude runtime
2. Let the agent complete its task fully (claims issue, creates worktree, implements, merges, closes)
3. Claude CLI exits after finishing
4. Obelisk shows the agent as FAIL / Failed instead of DONE / Completed

## Expected Behavior

Agent should show as Completed (✓) with status DONE when the Claude process exits with code 0.

## Actual Behavior

Agent shows as Failed (✗) with status FAIL, even though the process exited successfully.

## Investigation Notes

The issue is likely in how `AgentExited` events are handled. The exit watcher detects process termination and may not be correctly distinguishing between exit code 0 (success) and non-zero (failure). Or the PTY reader hitting EOF may be racing with the exit code check, causing the status to be set to Failed before the exit code is evaluated.

Check:
- `runtime.rs` — exit watcher task, how exit status is captured
- `app.rs` / `main.rs` — `AgentExited` event handling, how status is set based on exit code
- Whether ConPTY on Windows reports exit codes differently than Unix PTY

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent with exit code 0 marked as Completed
- [ ] Agent with non-zero exit code marked as Failed
- [ ] Works correctly on Windows (ConPTY) and Unix
title: Claude agent exit code 0 incorrectly reported as failed

close_reason: Not a real gap - DIAGNOSTICS panel already shows status, elapsed, lines, rate, runtime, task, priority
description: Show per-agent metrics like tokens/sec, time elapsed, lines of output, and exit codes in a summary view.
title: Add agent runtime metrics dashboard

description:
## Summary

Enable multi-stage agent pipelines where agents execute sequentially on the same issue, each with a different role. For example: a design agent writes the plan, an implementation agent executes it, and a review agent validates the result.

## Motivation

Complex issues benefit from separation of concerns. A single agent trying to design, implement, test, and review produces worse results than specialized agents each doing one thing well. Pipelines turn obelisk from a parallel task runner into a full development workflow engine.

## Proposed Behavior

- Define pipeline stages via issue labels or a new config (e.g., `pipeline:design,implement,review`)
- Each stage spawns an agent with a stage-specific prompt template
- Previous stage output (or a summary) is passed as context to the next stage
- Pipeline progress visualized in agent list (e.g., "Stage 2/3: IMPLEMENT")
- Failed stage blocks subsequent stages with option to retry or skip
- Pipeline can mix runtimes/models per stage (e.g., haiku for design, opus for implement)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Pipeline definition mechanism (labels, config, or CLI)
- [ ] Sequential stage execution with context passing
- [ ] Per-stage prompt templates
- [ ] Pipeline progress indicator in UI
- [ ] Stage failure handling (retry/skip/abort)
- [ ] Mixed runtime/model support per stage
title: Pipeline mode: chain agents sequentially

close_reason: Completed: Added toggleable help overlay (press '?' to open/close, Esc also closes). Overlay lists all keybindings organized by view (Dashboard, Agent Detail, Event Log) and mode (Interactive vs Observe). Added '?' hint to the bottom keybindings bar in all views.
description: A keybindings bar exists at the bottom of the screen, but there is no full help overlay/modal (e.g. press '?' to show all shortcuts). Add a toggleable help screen that lists all keybindings organized by view (Dashboard, Agent Detail, Event Log) and mode (Interactive vs Observe).
title: Add keyboard shortcut help overlay

description:
## Summary

Add a new "On Deck" panel below (or alongside) the Ready Queue in the Dashboard that shows issues which have exactly one unresolved dependency remaining. These are the issues that will become ready next — giving you a preview of upcoming work.

## Motivation

The Ready Queue shows what's actionable now, but there's no visibility into what's coming next. If you're planning which agents to prioritize, knowing that completing issue X will unblock three more issues is valuable signal. The On Deck panel turns the dependency graph into actionable intelligence.

## Proposed Behavior

- New panel in Dashboard view, positioned below the Ready Queue (or as a toggleable tab)
- Shows issues where all dependencies are resolved except one
- Each entry shows: priority, type, title, AND the blocking issue ID/title
- Format: "[P1] [feature] Add merge queue — blocked by: obelisk-0da (Auto-create PR...)"
- Sorted by priority (highest first), then by how close the blocker is to completion
- Keybinding to toggle On Deck visibility (e.g., `d` or panel cycling)
- When a blocking issue is completed, the newly-ready issue animates/highlights as it moves up to the Ready Queue

## Design Considerations

- Query: `bd list` with dependency analysis — find issues with exactly 1 open dep
- Could also show a count badge on Ready Queue: "READY [5] | ON DECK [3]"
- Alternative name: "Up Next", "Warming Up", "Almost Ready"
- If terminal is too small, On Deck collapses or hides gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] On Deck panel showing issues with exactly 1 remaining dependency
- [ ] Blocking issue shown inline for each entry
- [ ] Priority-sorted display
- [ ] Toggle keybinding
- [ ] Responsive layout (collapse when space is tight)
- [ ] Visual transition when issue moves from On Deck → Ready
title: On Deck panel: show issues one dependency away from ready

close_reason: Completed: Live git diff preview panel in agent detail view. Toggle with 'd' key, auto-refreshes every 3s, color-coded diff rendering (green/red), file summary header, scrollable via J/K, graceful worktree handling.
description:
## Summary

Add a toggleable panel in the Agent Detail view that shows the real-time git diff of changes the agent is making in its worktree. Instead of reading PTY output and guessing what's happening, you see the actual code changes as they land.

## Motivation

Right now you're watching raw terminal output scroll by. That's useful for understanding the agent's thought process, but what you really care about is: what did it actually change? A live diff view gives you that at a glance — catch bad edits early, understand scope creep, and build confidence that the agent is on track.

## Proposed Behavior

- New keybinding (e.g., `d`) in Agent Detail toggles a side panel or overlay showing `git diff` from the agent's worktree
- Auto-refreshes on a short interval (2-5s) or on detected file writes
- Syntax-highlighted diff output (additions green, deletions red)
- Shows file-level summary at top (files changed, insertions, deletions)
- Scrollable independently from PTY output
- Only available for agents with active worktrees

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Keybinding toggles diff panel in Agent Detail view
- [ ] Diff pulled from agent's worktree path via `git diff` + `git diff --cached`
- [ ] Auto-refresh on interval
- [ ] Color-coded diff rendering (green/red)
- [ ] File summary header
- [ ] Graceful handling when worktree doesn't exist yet
title: Live git diff preview in agent detail view

close_reason: Completed: config-loaded max_concurrent now clamped to 1-20 range. +/- keybindings already enforced 1-20. Resolved pre-existing merge conflict in ui.rs adding WorktreeOverview to both compact and non-compact view matches.
description:

The max concurrent agent slots should cap at 20. Verify the current upper bound enforced by the +/- keybindings and update if needed.

title: Increase max concurrent agent limit to 20

description:
## Summary

Show count badges in the tab bar next to each view name. Dashboard shows ready/active counts, Event Log shows unread count, History shows session count.

## Proposed Behavior

- Tab bar currently: [1:DASHBOARD | 2:AGENTS | 3:EVENT LOG | 4:HISTORY]
- With badges: [1:DASHBOARD (5/3) | 2:AGENTS | 3:EVENT LOG (12) | 4:HISTORY (8)]
- Dashboard: ready count / active agent count
- Event Log: number of events since last viewed (resets when you switch to that tab)
- History: total session count
- Badges dim or disappear when zero

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Count badges rendered in tab headers
- [ ] Dashboard shows ready/active counts
- [ ] Event Log shows unread-since-last-visit count
- [ ] History shows session count
- [ ] Zero counts hidden or dimmed
title: Tab bar badges with counts

description:
## Summary

Obelisk has no README. Create a complete, descriptive README.md that covers what the project is, how to install and run it, all features, keybindings, views, configuration, and the agent workflow.

## Should Include

- Project overview — what obelisk is and why it exists
- Screenshots or ASCII mockups of the TUI views
- Installation / build instructions (Rust/Cargo)
- Prerequisites (bd CLI, git, AI runtimes like claude/codex/copilot)
- Quick start guide
- Full keybinding reference table (all views)
- Description of each view (Dashboard, Agent Detail, Event Log, History)
- Agent lifecycle explanation (spawn, monitor, retry, kill, dismiss)
- Ready queue sorting and filtering
- Auto-spawn mode
- Runtime and model selection
- Worktree management
- Session persistence
- Desktop notifications
- Configuration options

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] README.md exists at repo root
- [ ] Covers all major features and views
- [ ] Keybinding reference table for all views
- [ ] Build and run instructions
- [ ] Prerequisites documented
title: Create comprehensive README for obelisk

close_reason: Completed: Split-pane multi-agent view with 2-up/4-up layout (key 5), focus switching (Tab), pin/unpin (g), scrolling, auto-fill from running agents, responsive layout based on terminal width. Status header per pane shows agent ID, status, elapsed time, phase.
description:
## Summary

Add a new view (or mode within Agent Detail) that shows 2-4 agent PTY outputs side by side in split panes. Monitor multiple agents simultaneously without tabbing between them.

## Motivation

When you've got 5+ agents running, flipping between them with ←/→ is slow. You lose context switching between agents. A split view lets you keep eyes on the agents that matter — the one that's stuck, the one that's almost done, and the one working on the critical path — all at once.

## Proposed Behavior

- New view (e.g., key `5` or toggle in Agent Detail) showing 2-4 panes
- Each pane shows a miniaturized agent PTY output with status header
- Pane layout: 2-up (50/50 horizontal) or 4-up (2x2 grid)
- One pane is "focused" (highlighted border) for scrolling
- Tab or number keys to switch focus between panes
- Panes auto-assigned to running agents or manually pinnable
- Responsive — adapts to terminal size, hides panes if too narrow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Split-pane view with 2 or 4 agent outputs
- [ ] Focus switching between panes
- [ ] Status header per pane (agent ID, status, elapsed)
- [ ] Responsive layout based on terminal dimensions
- [ ] Manual pin/unpin agents to panes
- [ ] Scrolling works in focused pane
notes: COMPLETED: Split-pane multi-agent view. Added View::SplitPane (key 5) with 2-up/4-up layout, focus cycling (Tab), pin/unpin (g), scrolling, auto-fill from running agents. Also resolved merge conflicts between search (obelisk-0l4) and diff panel (obelisk-px8). DECISIONS: Used separate render_vt100_screen_plain for split panes (no search highlighting in split view for simplicity). Pin state stored on AgentInstance.
title: Split-pane multi-agent view

close_reason: Completed: Added age badges to ready queue list entries with color-coded thresholds (<1d neutral, 1-3d yellow, 3-7d orange, 7d+ red). Added created_at field to BeadTask struct.
description:
## Summary

Color-code issue titles or age badges in the ready queue based on how long they've been open. Fresh issues are neutral, aging issues shift toward warm colors. Stale work becomes visually obvious.

## Proposed Behavior

- Calculate age from issue created_at timestamp
- Color bands: <1d neutral, 1-3d yellow, 3-7d orange, 7d+ red
- Applied as a subtle background tint or age badge (e.g., "3d") next to the issue
- Configurable thresholds would be nice but hardcoded is fine for v1

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Age calculated from issue creation date
- [ ] Color applied based on age thresholds
- [ ] Visible in ready queue list entries
notes: COMPLETED: Added age badge with color coding to ready queue. Added created_at field to BeadTask. Age badge shows Xd/Xh with color bands: <1d muted, 1-3d yellow, 3-7d orange, 7d+ red. DECISIONS: Hardcoded thresholds for v1 as spec allows.
title: Issue age color coding in ready queue

description:
## Summary

Add a small "Recent Completions" feed to the Dashboard showing the last N issues that were closed, with their close reason, runtime used, and how long they took. A rolling scoreboard of wins.

## Proposed Behavior

- Compact list panel showing last 5-10 completed issues (from current + recent sessions)
- Each entry: issue ID, title (truncated), runtime/model, duration, close status (success/fail)
- Color-coded: green for success, red for failed
- Auto-scrolls as new completions come in
- Positioned below or alongside the event log in Dashboard
- Data sourced from session history + current session agent completions

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Feed showing recent completed issues
- [ ] Includes runtime, model, duration, and outcome
- [ ] Color-coded success/failure
- [ ] Auto-updates on agent completion
- [ ] Sourced from session persistence data
notes: COMPLETED: CompletionRecord struct, recent_completions VecDeque in App, seeded from history sessions, populated on agent exit, rendered as 3-column Dashboard bottom panel with color-coded success/failure. DECISIONS: Used 3-column bottom layout (throughput 25% | completions 45% | events 30%) to position alongside event log per spec.
title: Recent completions feed panel
